prompt: Extract a full structured requirements spec from the Plane project management tool. The GitHub repo is at https://github.com/makeplane/plane — read the README and any architecture or feature docs you can find there. I want Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints clearly separated.

assertions.text: Output contains all four sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
Mission section contains a single sentence or short statement (not a list), expressing the core invariant purpose
Goals section contains specific, measurable outcomes (e.g. feature capabilities, user actions) rather than vague aspirations
Constraints section captures actual hard boundaries (e.g. deployment model, license, tech stack, integrations) distinct from goals
Output references specific content from the Plane GitHub repo (e.g. specific features, project structure, technology choices) — not just generic PM tool boilerplate
At least one Premise or Constraint is implicit (not stated word-for-word in the source) — e.g. assumptions about users, deployment environment, or integration ecosystem

Output shows evidence of bottom-up Chain of Knowledge expansion with multiple levels visible (e.g. L5 Topics through L1 Domains, or equivalent layered knowledge build-up from raw facts to domain-level synthesis)
Every constraint (both hard and soft) cites its source — who or what imposed it (e.g. 'MIT license', 'README', 'architecture doc', 'inferred from tech stack') — not just the constraint text alone
